theme: frontier kingdom tactics campaign villages keeps capitals and commander led square grid warfare

entities.facing_rule: GameView rotates the pennant tip and front marker by unit faceX and faceY after movement and attacks
entities.animation_rule: GameView lerps unit tokens between tiles, pulses attack arcs, flashes damage, and expands capture or commander-order rings instead of leaving static markers
entities.movement_rule: selected player units advance by reachable tile paths on the square grid while preserving facing toward travel or target direction

entities.facing_rule: GameView mirrors the directional pennant marker toward AI movement and counterattack direction
entities.animation_rule: AI units share the animated token system, including move interpolation, attack arcs, hit flashes, and capture pulses
entities.movement_rule: enemy AI chooses move cells, attacks, and captures through the same tile-grid movement rules as the player

entities.facing_rule: the heir token follows movement or danger direction and uses the same render anchor as allied units
entities.animation_rule: the escort token shares movement lerp and damage flash behavior and highlights the exit state through a bright ring pulse
entities.movement_rule: escort chapters advance the heir on the same board grid until the unit reaches the exit tiles on the far edge

entities.facing_rule: terrain is static while ownership glow and capture pulse communicate state changes
entities.animation_rule: GameView draws shared tile bitmaps when present, falls back to color-coded terrain fills, and overlays ownership pulses for keeps forts villages and capitals
entities.movement_rule: terrain sets movement cost defense bonuses capture eligibility and exit logic for escort chapters

entities.animation_rule: GameView crops a pixel-art strip from the shared roguelike sheet and places it inside the board frame as a tactical command texture

required_runtime_checks: primary moving entities have idle and move states
attacking entities have attack states with visible arcs or timing pulses
damageable entities flash or otherwise communicate hit feedback
defeated units are removed from the live board roster
directional entities define default_facing and use the facing_rule during movement and attacks
shared tile assets are loaded through AssetManager and BitmapFactory from game_art paths
both assigned shared gameplay art packs are used by the running game
anchors and hitboxes match tile-center interactions on the board
capture ownership changes produce a distinct pulse state before the next turn
